events.name: San Antonio Spurs at Indiana Pacers

headlines.description: — De’Aaron Fox scored 24 points and the San Antonio Spurs won their first game since Victor Wembanyama injured his left knee, beating the Indiana 123-113 on Friday night for the Pacers' 11th straight loss.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs beat free-falling Pacers 123-113 without Wembanyama

headlines.description: — Justin Champagnie scored a season-high 20 points and the Washington Wizards continued their recent improvement with a 119-99 victory over the Brooklyn Nets on Friday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Champagnie scores season-high 20 points to lead the Wizards to a 119-99 win over the Nets

headlines.description: — Donovan Mitchell scored 33 points and Darius Garland had 18 points and eight assists as the Cleveland Cavaliers beat the short-handed Denver Nuggets 113-108 on Friday night.

events.name: Atlanta Hawks at New York Knicks

headlines.description: — Jalen Johnson had 18 points, 11 assists and 10 rebounds in his seventh triple-double of the season, leading the Atlanta Hawks to a 111-99 victory over the New York Knicks on Friday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Johnson has another triple-double and the Hawks beat the Knicks 111-99

headlines.description: — Matas Buzelis scored 17 of his 21 points in the second half, Kevin Huerter added 20 points, and the Chicago Bulls rallied to beat the Orlando Magic 121-114 on Friday night for their second straight win.
headlines.shortLinkText: Buzelis scores 21 points, Huerter adds 20 as Bulls beat Magic 121-114 for 2nd straight win

headlines.description: — Giannis Antetokounmpo dunked on a lob from Kevin Porter Jr. with 4.7 seconds remaining to give the Milwaukee Bucks a 122-121 victory over the Charlotte Hornets on Friday night.

headlines.description: — Deni Avdija had 34 points and 11 assists and the Portland Trail Blazers beat the injury-depleted New Orleans Pelicans 122-09 on Friday night.

events.name: Oklahoma City Thunder at Golden State Warriors

headlines.description: — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 30 points and seven assists, and the Oklahoma City Thunder dominated the short-handed Golden State Warriors for a 131-94 win Friday night.

events.name: Memphis Grizzlies at Los Angeles Lakers

headlines.description: — Luka Doncic scored 34 points — half on free throws — and LeBron James added 31 as the Los Angeles Lakers rallied to beat the short-handed Memphis Grizzlies 128-121 on Friday night to open a two-game set.
headlines.shortLinkText: Doncic and James lead Lakers' rally to beat shorthanded Grizzlies 128-121
